hero.title: Digital Agents that work while you are away.

They respond, qualify, schedule, follow up, and convert inquiries into sales.
No rest. No errors. No missed opportunities.
hero.cta: I want to see how they work in my business

problem.title:
Most companies don't lose customers due to lack of interest.
They lose them due to lack of response.
problem.text:
A customer enters your website.
Doubts.
Asks.

If they don't receive an immediate response, they leave.

If they receive a slow response, they talk to the competition.

If there is no follow-up, they forget about you.

This isn't marketing.
It's a system.

And if you don't have a system, you have invisible losses every week.
problem.cta: I want to detect my losses

definition.title:
A digital agent is not a friendly chat.
It's a work system.
definition.text:
A digital agent is a tool that:
• Responds to common questions
• Qualifies potential customers
• Schedules visits or calls
• Sends information automatically
• Follows up if there is no response
• Recovers contacts who dropped out halfway

All this without you having to be on top of it.

24 hours a day.

items.title: Text Agent
items.how_it_works: The customer asks. The agent responds clearly. If it detects real interest, it qualifies. If it fits, it schedules directly.
items.result: Less manual work and more speed.

items.title: Voice Agent
items.ideal_for: Inbound calls, Appointment confirmation, Reminders, Follow-up
items.how_it_works: Responds to basic questions, schedules visits, confirms appointments, and collects data before a salesperson calls.
items.result: Fewer missed calls, more real opportunities.

items.title: Follow-up Agent
items.how_it_works: Reminds about sent quotes, reactivates contacts, sends personalized messages, and detects latent interest.

items.title: Corporate Avatar (optional)
items.ideal_for: Explaining services, Showing products, Presenting information, Demonstrations
items.how_it_works: A visible, coherent, and constant face that brings trust and consistency.

items.title: Industrial
points: Gives control to the sales department

questions: Does your company clearly explain what it does and for whom?
Does a customer understand in less than 5 seconds what benefit they get?
Does your website have a clear result-oriented message?
Does your website convey professionalism and trust?
Does the page load fast on mobile?
Does the visitor know what step to take next?
Does your website have simple forms to capture leads?
Do you have a visible WhatsApp button?
Do you offer any free resource to attract contacts?
Do you respond to leads in less than 5 minutes?
Do you have an automatic initial response system?
Can your company respond outside business hours?
Are all contacts organized in a CRM?
Do you know where each lead comes from?
Do you classify leads according to interest or customer type?
Can customers easily book a meeting online?
Do you send automatic appointment reminders?
Is there a clear process from contact to meeting?
Do you follow up on sent quotes?
Do you contact leads with pending response again?
Does your team know which leads are most likely to buy?
Are there repetitive tasks that could be automated?
Do you use AI to answer frequently asked questions?
Do you have automations to send information or documents?
Do you measure how many leads actually become customers?
